checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully represents the text found in the source PDF. The section headings, list items, and paragraph text in the provided sections (Title, Agenda, Accessible Forms, Forms in MS Word, Forms in Adobe Acrobat, Radio buttons, Preview Form, Step 6, Get in touch) align with the content found on the corresponding pages of the source. The title in the metadata is AI-generated, which is an allowed change. The tables included in the JSON are structural extractions of the form fields shown in the screenshots within the document, which is a standard accessibility enhancement and does not constitute fabrication of information. No invented body text or unrelated sections were found.

The contact information provided in the final section, 'Get in touch' (page 34), was verified. The phone number '833-294-2257' in the output matches the phone number in the source. No other contact information (URLs or emails) is present in the output's text elements to compare, and no fabricated information was inserted.

While several pages and sections (e.g., pages 4, 5, 8, 11-13, 15-33) are not present in the provided JSON, they are explicitly marked as omitted due to a 'token budget' placeholder. Within the sections that were provided, the document structure follows the source. Therefore, no sections were silently dropped by the conversion process itself.

The tables in the output correspond to form fields and software properties shown in screenshots throughout the document. The row counts for the 'Billing Contact' (page 6), 'Voter Registration Form' (page 7), 'Radio Button Properties' (page 9), and 'Adobe Acrobat Form Preview' (page 10) tables were compared against the visual information in the source images. The tables accurately capture the relevant data fields, with minor omissions (like the Date of Birth field in the Preview section) being considered acceptable reformatting as the data is captured in other redundant tables (e.g., in the Step 6 section).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In the 'Get in touch' section (page 34), significant contact information is missing. The source lists 'Web: www.ablr360.com', 'Call: 833-294-2257', and 'Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com'. The output only includes the 'Ablr' name and the phone number. Out of the six lines of text in this section (Heading, Name, Web, Phone, Email, Closing), two lines (33%) are omitted. Per the guidelines, an omission of more than 25% of a section's text is a major defect.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording across all provided sections was checked for accuracy. The output preserves the meaning and intent of the source text. Punctuation and whitespace normalization are within acceptable limits.

checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain any inappropriately duplicated content. Text blocks and images appear in the JSON corresponding to their presence in the source slides. The repetition of the company logo reflects its placement on multiple slides in the source (Pages 1, 3, 13, 34).

The heading hierarchy is normalized, with the title of each slide generally being assigned an H1 level. This is consistent across the document and represents a logical sectioning for a presentation-style source. There are no skipped heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

The tables in the output are generated from form fields and property dialogs shown in screenshots within the source PDF. These tables (e.g., in Sections 6, 7, 9, 14) are structurally sound, with appropriate headers ('Field', 'Value') and rows that correctly pair labels with their associated data or empty values.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the slides from top to bottom. Elements within individual slides, including headings, paragraphs, and lists, are ordered correctly. Tables extracted from images are placed immediately following the related text, which is an acceptable optimization.

Several numbered lists in the source document were incorrectly converted into unordered lists in the JSON output. This occurs on multiple pages where the source explicitly uses '1.', '2.', etc., to indicate a sequence of steps.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6: '1. Use underline', '2. Can use...'; Page 7: '1. Enter field name' through '4. Save time...'; Page 9: '1. Add radio buttons' through '5. Enter Choice...'; Page 10: '1. Use Preview button' through '3. Select Clear Form'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 6 elements: list_type: 'unordered'; Section 7 elements: list_type: 'unordered'; Section 9 elements: list_type: 'unordered'; Section 10 elements: list_type: 'unordered'.

checks.a_analysis: The contact links (web address and email address) present on the final slide of the source PDF (Page 34) are completely missing from the output document. While the company name and phone number are present, the critical URL and email contact information were not captured.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 34 (last section) contains only the heading 'Get in touch', 'Thank You!', and paragraphs for 'Ablr' and 'Call: 833-294-2257'.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output exhibits major layout discrepancies where several information-rich slides from the PDF are rendered as nearly empty sections. In the source PDF, slides 15, 21, 29, 30, and 32 contain vital instructional text and screenshots. In the converted HTML, these sections consist almost entirely of headings with no following content (neither the text nor the images). This fundamentally alters the visual organization and flow of information, making the document feel incomplete and preventing a sighted user from finding the same information in the expected locations.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 15 ('Creating a Keyboard Shortcut') and Page 21 ('Enable Speech Viewer') contain headings, instructional text, and multiple screenshots.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML rendering of these sections shows only the headings ('Creating a Keyboard Shortcut' and 'Enable Speech Viewer') with no content following them before the next heading begins.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Creating a Keyboard Shortcut' (slide 15) and 'Enable Speech Viewer' (slide 21)

checks.a_analysis: The conversion engine extracts form data and settings from screenshots into HTML tables to improve accessibility. These tables, such as 'Billing Contact' (slide 6) and 'NVDA Settings: Speech' (slide 19), are rendered clearly with defined borders and aligned columns. They are visually sound and accurately represent the structured data found in the original images.

Numerous meaningful images—specifically screenshots of software interfaces, settings dialogs, and keyboards—are missing from the HTML output. These images are central to the document's purpose of instructing users on how to use accessibility tools. Missing meaningful images were identified for slides 15, 18, 19, 20, 21, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, and 32. While some slides have their content extracted into tables, the original visual context is lost, and for many other slides, no content at all is rendered where images should be.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 30 ('Enabling VoiceOver iDevices') contains four screenshots illustrating iOS accessibility settings.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output under the 'Enabling VoiceOver iDevices' heading contains only a repeated text placeholder and no images.

checks.a_analysis: No significant instances of body content duplication (repeated paragraphs or tables) were found. While slide titles appear as headings in both the navigation area and the main body, this is expected behavior. The extraction of table data alongside the original image is considered an accessibility feature rather than visual duplication of content blocks.
